subject: Dead parkway elm on Hirsch Ave needs to come down
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, there's a large dead elm in the parkway right in front of 2109 Hirsch Ave that's been dead since at least last fall. The bark is falling off in chunks and two big branches already came down during a windstorm last month. I'm genuinely worried it's going to drop a limb on someone walking to school. Who do I contact to get this removed before something bad happens? Happy to let crews access my side yard if needed.

subject: Dead parkway elm on Hirsch Ave needs to come down
body_clean: Ms. Reyes, thank you for flagging this. I've logged the dead elm at 2109 Hirsch Ave as a priority removal. Our arborist will conduct a hazard assessment by end of this week, and if he confirms imminent risk the crew will be scheduled within 10 business days. We'll post a 48-hour notice on the tree before work begins. Side-yard access appreciated—I'll note it on the work order. I'll send a follow-up once the removal date is confirmed.

subject: Storm limbs blocking sidewalk on 25th Ave
body_clean: Hi Mayor, the big storms Tuesday night took down some serious limbs from a parkway cottonwood at 25th Ave near St. Charles. They're piled over the whole sidewalk—kids and strollers have to walk in the street to get around them. Public Works recorded a voicemail from me Wednesday but I haven't heard back. Can someone get out there? The wood is still green so it's heavy—this isn't a one-man chainsaw job.

subject: Storm limbs blocking sidewalk on 25th Ave
body_clean: Mr. Webb, apologies for the voicemail lag—storm calls stacked up Tuesday night and Wednesday was non-stop. I'm sending a crew to 25th and St. Charles this afternoon to clear the downed limbs. We'll chip what we can on-site and haul the rest. If the parent trunk shows additional cracking we'll flag it for the arborist. Sidewalk should be clear by end of day.

subject: Parkway ash trees on Marshall Ave — EAB damage?
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I've lived on the 1700 block of Marshall Ave for 22 years and I'm very worried about the row of ash trees in front of our homes. I've been reading about the Emerald Ash Borer and these trees have the S-shaped bark galleries and D-shaped exit holes the university extension site describes. Several crowns are already thinning noticeably. Who at the Village handles this? I'd like someone qualified to take a look before spring when the borers become active again.

subject: Parkway ash trees on Marshall Ave — EAB damage?
body_clean: Mr. Wierzbicki, your concern is well founded—EAB has hit many Cook County communities hard. I've scheduled our certified arborist to walk the 1700 block of Marshall Ave in the next two weeks for a formal assessment. Trees showing more than 50% canopy dieback will be prioritized for removal; healthier specimens may qualify for our preventive treatment program depending on available funding. I'll mail results to all affected addresses once the assessment is complete.

subject: Sidewalk completely buckled by tree roots — Eastern Ave
body_clean: Mayor, I submitted this through the online form last fall and never got a response so I'm trying email. The parkway maple at 418 Eastern Ave has lifted and cracked a 15-foot section of sidewalk so badly that it's basically impassable for anyone using a walker or wheelchair. My mother-in-law nearly fell there in October. The crack at the worst point is at least 3 inches high. This is a trip hazard and a genuine ADA issue. Please have someone look at it.

subject: Overgrown parkway tree scraping my roof every time the wind blows
body_clean: Mayor, the parkway silver maple in front of 732 Bellwood Ave has branches that hang well over my roof and every time there's a stiff wind they scrape across my shingles. I had a roofer out last year who told me there's already some damage from the rubbing. The Village owns the parkway tree so I figured I should reach out before I start having water problems. Is there a way to request a trim through the Village?

subject: Ice storm took down big limb — Geneva Ave — need it cleared
body_clean: Good afternoon Mayor. The ice storm last Thursday snapped a major limb from the parkway oak at 88 Geneva Ave. It's resting on the fence between my yard and my neighbor's and pressing down hard—I'm afraid the fence posts are going to give. It also blocked the sidewalk for a day until I dragged some of it aside, but I couldn't move the main piece. Can a Public Works crew come remove it? I don't want to make things worse by touching the rest of it.

subject: Parkway tree growing into ComEd lines — Harvard Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing about the parkway elm at 2254 Harvard Ave. Several large branches are growing into the ComEd overhead lines and drooping lower every year. During last month's windy stretch a branch was swaying and actually touching the service drop to my house. ComEd told me it's a Village tree and to contact the Village. Who can I escalate this to? I'd rather not wait for an outage to get action.

subject: Request for new parkway tree plantings — Rice Ave
body_clean: Hi Mayor Okonkwo, I'm a longtime resident on the 2300 block of Rice Ave and just want to say the tree-planting the Village did on Washington Blvd a few years back is beautiful. Our block has almost no parkway trees—a lot were taken out over the years and never replaced. The summer sun on this stretch is brutal for kids at the bus stop. Is there any program we can get on to request new plantings? We'd even help with watering if the Village provides the trees.

subject: Tree stump still in parkway 8 months after removal — 50th Ave
body_clean: Mayor, I want to be nice about this because I know crews are busy, but a tree was removed from the parkway in front of 1614 50th Ave last December and the stump is still sitting there. It's become a trip hazard since the grass has grown up around it and you can't see it until you're already stumbling. Can we get a grinder out there to finish the job? I assumed it would happen in the spring but here we are in August.

subject: Hanging dead branch over sidewalk — Morris Ave
body_clean: Mayor, there's a large dead branch on the parkway tree in front of 909 Morris Ave that's been hanging half-broken for weeks. It's about 20 feet up, attached at maybe 30 percent, and it's directly over the sidewalk. Every time I walk under it I think today's the day. December winds have been something else this year. Can Public Works please come look at it as soon as possible—this is not a wait-three-weeks situation.

subject: Hanging dead branch over sidewalk — Morris Ave
body_clean: Mr. Ramirez, agreed—a widow-maker hanging over a public sidewalk doesn't wait. I'm dispatching the crew with the bucket truck this afternoon. If conditions are safe they'll drop it today; if road ice makes positioning unsafe we'll rope it off and return first thing tomorrow. I'll confirm once it's down.

subject: Parkway tree trimming request — Bohland Ave
body_clean: Hi Mayor, the parkway tree at 2817 Bohland Ave hasn't been trimmed in what looks like at least five or six years. The canopy is so dense it's blocking the street light and our block is noticeably darker than the rest of the street. There's also a low branch that a tall person has to duck under on the sidewalk. Not dangerous yet but it will be. Is there a way to request a trim through the Village?

subject: Parkway oak dropping large branches without warning — 19th Ave
body_clean: Hello, I'm writing because the parkway oak at 312 19th Ave is scaring me. Over the last two summers, three large branches have fallen in the yard and street with no warning—not during storms, just random warm days. My kids play out front and I feel like I have to watch that tree constantly. A neighbor mentioned oak wilt. Whatever the cause, I think it needs a professional assessment urgently. I don't want to wait for someone to get hurt.

subject: Ash tree in front of my house never leafed out — St. Paul Ave
body_clean: Hi, I'm new to Bellwood—just bought at 47 St. Paul Ave in February. The parkway ash in front of my house never leafed out this spring. Branches look completely bare. My neighbor says it was looking bad last fall too. I called the number on the Village website but got sent to voicemail twice. Who handles tree removal requests and how long does it typically take once it's approved?

subject: Parkway tree roots cracking curb — Granville Ave
body_clean: Mayor, the parkway maple at 1908 Granville Ave has roots that have cracked and displaced a 4-foot section of curb. Water pools there every rain and freezes in winter. I tripped on the raised section last week—nothing broke but I'm 68 and it rattled me. This tree needs root-pruning or the curb has to be rebuilt around it. Either way I wanted you to know it's become dangerous.

subject: Compliment for the tree crew on Monroe St — great work
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I just wanted to take a moment to say thank you. The tree crew that came through Monroe St last week did a beautiful job. They were polite, they cleaned up every chip, and honestly the block looks so much better. I've lived here 14 years and this is the best the parkway trees have ever looked. Please pass along our appreciation to the crew and whoever scheduled the work.

subject: Parkway pine blocking intersection sightline — 25th Ave and Madison
body_clean: Dear Mayor, the large pine tree in the parkway at the corner of 25th Ave and Madison St has grown so wide that it blocks the sightline for drivers turning from Madison onto 25th. I've nearly been hit twice this winter because oncoming cars can't see me until I'm already in the intersection. I think the lower branches need to be raised significantly or the whole tree may need to come out for safety. Can someone evaluate this intersection?
